title: Find Minimum in Rotated Sorted Array
description:
Take an ascending array of distinct integers and rotate it some unknown number of times between 1 and its length (so `[1,2,3,4,5]` might become `[3,4,5,1,2]`). Given only the rotated result `nums`, work out its smallest value -- and do it in logarithmic time rather than scanning the whole array.

**Example**

```
nums = [3, 4, 5, 1, 2]  ->  1
nums = [4, 5, 6, 7, 0, 1, 2]  ->  0
```
hints: Compare nums[mid] to nums[right]. If nums[mid] > nums[right], the minimum must be somewhere in mid+1..right (the rotation point is to the right of mid); otherwise the minimum is at mid or to its left.
Since values are distinct, you never need an equality tiebreak: nums[mid] can never equal nums[right] unless mid == right.

solutions.name: Binary search on the rotation point
solutions.explanation: Binary search for the pivot by comparing the midpoint to the rightmost remaining element. If nums[mid] is greater than nums[right], the array is still 'rotated' within mid+1..right, so the minimum lies strictly to the right of mid. Otherwise nums[mid..right] is already sorted ascending, so the minimum is at mid or somewhere to its left, and we can safely keep mid in the search range. The loop ends when left == right, which is the index of the minimum.
